screen.name: screen1
screen.title: Bullet from crime scene gun – Class characteristics
screen.desc: What is the caliber of this bullet? Use the scale to measure the caliber.
screen.response: Correct – this bullet’s diameter is 9 millimeters.
Remember, the caliber of a bullet is determined by its diameter. Try again.
That’s not what I see. Try again.

examples.title: Bullet from crime scene gun – Class characteristics
examples.desc:
Most modern guns have spiral grooves inside their barrels, which are called RIFLING.
When a bullet is fired, rifling makes the bullet spin in the air. Rifling leaves impressions on the surface of the bullet, which can either be conventional or polygonal.
examples.popup: Conventional rifling leaves deep, easy to see grooves on the surface of the bullet.
Polygonal rifling leaves impressions that are less easy to see, with less distinct grooves.

screen.name: screen2
screen.title: Bullet from crime scene gun – Class characteristics
screen.desc: What type of rifling impressions does this bullet have?
screen.response: No, polygonal rifling leaves grooves that are not that distinct. Try again.
No, rifling is either conventional or polygonal, never both. Try again.

screen.name: screen3
screen.title: Bullet from crime scene gun - Class characteristics
screen.desc: How many rifling impressions does this bullet have?
screen.caption: Previous Groove
Next Groove
screen.response: Not quite. Try rotating the bullet if you’re not sure and try again.
Not quite. Try rotating the bullet if you’re not sure and try again.

screen.name: screen4
screen.title: CLASS CHARACTERISTICS - SUMMARY
screen.response: Are you sure? All three class characteristics need to be the same to confirm a match. Try again.
